citRespParty.rpOrgName: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

dataIdInfo.idAbs:
This metadata report applies to the 2018 ADF&G Salmon Subdistricts. Subdistricts are regulatory areas that divide the Waters of Alaska and the adjacent Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) into small units for the purpose of fisheries management.

This project received funding under award NA15NMF4270272 from NOAA Fisheries Service, in cooperation with the Saltonstall-Kennedy Grant Program. The statements, findings, conclusions, and recommendations are those of the author(s) and do not necessarily reflect the views of NOAA Fisheries

THE STATE OF ALASKA, DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND GAME, MAKES NO EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES (INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS) WITH RESPECT TO THE ACCURACY, CHARACTER, FUNCTION, OR CAPABILITIES OF THE DATA, SERVICES, OR PRODUCTS OR THEIR APPROPRIATENESS FOR ANY PARTICULAR PURPOSE. The Department of Fish and Game is not liable for any direct, incidental, indirect, special, compensatory, consequential or other damages suffered by the user or any other person or entity from the use of or failure of the data, services, or products even if the Department of Fish and Game has been advised of the possibility of such potential loss or damage. The entire risk as to the results of the use of the data, services, or products is assumed by the user.

Data may be subject to periodic change without prior notification. To ensure receipt of the most current information, please refer requests to the ADF&G Open Data website. Users may not reproduce or distribute these data without explicit consent of the Alaska Department of Fish and Game.

These data are provided for general visual reference and to aid users in generating various natural resource analyses and products. There are no constraints to accessing these data, other than the limitations or constraints associated with a specific dataset or set forth herein. It is strongly recommended that careful attention be paid to the contents of the metadata file associated with these data.

Any hardcopy or electronic products utilizing these data shall clearly indicate their source. If the user has modified the data in any way, the user must describe the modifications they have performed. The user agrees not to misrepresent these data, nor to imply that the Alaska Department of Fish and Game approved any changes they made.
dataIdInfo.idPurp:
Alaska's commercial fisheries are managed using various regulatory areas (i.e., districts and sections). This data set defines the current valid subdistricts that may be used in commercial salmon fisheries in 2018. This data set should NOT be used for navigation or for determining compliance with ADF&G Commercial Fishing Regulations. Please consult the ADF&G Salmon Commercial Fisheries Regulations for the official definitions of regulatory boundaries.
NOTE: These data attempt to depict boundaries as used for management during a specific time period. In some cases, boundaries used in practice may differ from those described in regulations, reports, maps, and other aids.
dataIdInfo.idCredit: Alaska Department of Fish and Game, Commercial Fisheries Division

searchKeys.keyword: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

otherKeys.keyword: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

dataExt.exDesc: These data were valid from of January1, 1963 to Present.

idPoC.rpOrgName: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

Consts.useLimit: These data are provided for general visual reference and to aid users in generating various natural resource analyses and products. There are no constraints to accessing these data, other than the limitations or constraints associated with a specific dataset or set forth herein. It is strongly recommended that careful attention be paid to the contents of the metadata file associated with these data.

scpExt.exDesc: This data quality report applies to the commercial salmon subdistrict polygon features and associated attributes published in the PVB_PWS_Subdistricts data set.

dataLineage.statement: The origin of this dataset is PVG_Statewide_1986 (originally named "ADF&G Groundfish/Shellfish Statistical Area Polygons 1986-2000"). Source data was originated by Tim Haverland (ADF&G) and published April 4, 1999.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1961_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1963_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1963 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 1963.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1963_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1977_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1977 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 1977.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1977_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1978_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1978 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 1978.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1978_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1979_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1979 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 1979.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1979_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1985_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1985 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 1985.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1985_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1987_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1987 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 1987.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1987_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1989_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1989 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 1989.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1989_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1991_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1991 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 1991.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1991_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1997_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1997 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 1997.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_1997_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2003_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2003 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 2003.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2003_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2006_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2006 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 2006.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2006_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2009_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2009 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 2009.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2009_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2012_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2012 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 2012.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2012_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2016_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2016 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 2016.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2016_GCS_WGS1984 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_Subistricts_2018_GCS_WGS1984. Polygons were merged by Subdistricts and were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2018 (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The purpose of this step was to create a layer that reflected the commercial salmon subdistricts in 2018.

dataSource.srcDesc: The source data for the coastline was "Alaska Coastline 1:63,360", published by the Alaska Department of Natural Resources, Land Records Section, on 02/11/1998.

report.measDesc: The Record Completeness measure is intended to ensure that all atrribute records are complete. This measure does not include data correctness, only record completion.
report.evalMethDesc: Attribute records were examined for completeness using the "Select by Attribute" method. Unique values should consist of one of the values described under the Fields section of this metadata.

ConResult.conExpl: All attibute records are recorded as appropriate (03/21/2018 S.Larsen).

report.measDesc: The Record Accuracy measure is intended to ensure that all attribute records are accurate.
report.evalMethDesc: Attribute records were examined for accuracy by regional staff.

ConResult.conExpl: All attibute records are recorded correctly (J. Botz 03/21/2018).

report.measDesc: The Polygon Accuracy measure is intended to ensure that subdistrict polygons are accurate.
report.evalMethDesc: Regulatory coordinates (e.g., Registration Areas, Districts, Subdistricts) were recorded from the appropriate Alaska Fish and Game Laws and Regulations, and plotted over the appropriate feature class.

enttyp.enttypd: Commercial Salmon Fishery Subdistricts valid as of 1/1/2018
enttyp.enttypds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: One of the four major fishery groups aggregated by species type.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A term used by the ADF&G to divide the management of commercial fishery resources in Alaska. Regions are divided on a state level and are the highest division of management in ADF&G.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A one letter code which refers to a physical area within an ADF&G Region. Registration Areas are used for commercial fishery management purposes.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A physical area defined in regulations, used for commercial fishery management. Districts are located within Registration Areas.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The name of a physical location described in regulations. Corresponds to the location that a commercial fishing vessel is registered to fish in.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A physical area defined in regulations, used for commercial fishery management. Subdistricts are located within Districts.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A code used to refer to a district.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A series of letters used to refer to a subdistrict.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: <Null> There are no Subdistrict Codes in this dataset.

attr.attrdef: Effective start date for a particular polygon. This field is intended to capture the effective start date for a given configuration of an area.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: Date corresponding to the day, month, and year that a particular configuration of an area was established. Usually corresponds to January 1st.

attr.attrdef: Effective end date for a particular polygon. This field is intended to capture the effective end date for a given configuration of an area.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: Date corresponding to the day, month, and year that a particular configuration of an area was retired. Usually corresponds to December 31st.

attr.attrdef: A one to two digit code used to refer to a specific GIS dataset series
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

mdContact.rpOrgName: Alaska Department of Fish and Game
